sourceText: Suggests that small observations or details can lead to great discoveries.
Failures in civil service examinations, setbacks in official career, inaccurate and confusing medical information, and the hardships during 27 years of extensive writing and field research.

Through 27 years of dedicated effort, traveling the country to personally collect and study herbs, and persistent writing, driven by a steadfast desire to aid the common people, he completed the 'Bencao Gangmu'.
All medicines in the world originate from water, and water is the source that sustains all things.

Emphasizes that the fundamental efficacy of medicines is connected to nature, particularly the life force of water, suggesting that understanding the principles of nature is fundamental to pharmacology.
The art of healing must be founded upon benevolence and righteousness.
Stresses that healing is not merely about curing diseases but must be based on human compassion and morality.
There is no end to learning; one must constantly explore the new.
Highlights the importance of acquiring knowledge and the necessity of continuous self-improvement.
Initial skepticism about the practicality of his ideas, difficulties in securing funding, and resistance from established scientific and industrial circles were significant hurdles.
Through persistent experimentation, demonstration, and the historic transatlantic wireless transmission, he proved the value of his technology and gained recognition for his innovation.
Science does not cheat us; it is we who cheat ourselves by experience.
This means that human experience or interpretation can distort scientific facts.
